data.title: Hang Seng Closes Shy Of 26,000-Point After Late Recovery
data.description:
Hong Kong equities staged a late-session recovery on April 24, erasing earlier losses to close higher as strength in technology and semiconductor shares offset weakness in autos.

The benchmark Hang Seng Index rose 0.24% to 25,978, adding 62 points, with total turnover reaching HK$236.7 billion as investors rotated back into growth names.

The Hang Seng Tech Index also edged up 0.75% to 4,902, supported by renewed buying interest in heavyweight tech stocks.

Semiconductor maker SMIC led the blue-chip gains, surging 10% to top the index performers, underscoring continued investor appetite for China’s chip sector.

Among internet giants, Alibaba gained 1%, while Baidu rose 0.7%, helping anchor the broader tech rebound.

However, gains were capped by weakness in the auto sector. Geely Automobile and Li Auto fell 2.7% and 4.4% respectively, both hitting 10-day lows amid ongoing demand concerns.

On the mainland, sentiment remained softer. The Shanghai Composite Index slipped 0.33% to 4,079, while the Shenzhen Component Index declined 0.69% to 14,940, reflecting continued caution in A-share markets.

Despite mixed regional performance, Hong Kong’s late tech-led rebound kept the Hang Seng just shy of the 26,000-point mark as investors weighed sector rotation against broader macro uncertainty.

data.title: Market Snapshot: Hong Kong Market Ends Higher On Renewed Investor Confidence
data.description:
Hong Kong stocks closed the week of April 20 to April 24, 2026, with solid gains, as renewed investor confidence and buying interest in technology and financial shares lifted the broader market.

The benchmark Hang Seng Index rose 1% over the week to finish at 18,742.56, gaining 185.34 points from 18,557.22 at the start of the trading period.

The market’s upward momentum was driven by strength in major tech counters and financial institutions, supported by improving sentiment across Asian equities and expectations of continued policy support from mainland China.

While some investors locked in profits toward the week’s end, overall sentiment remained constructive as bargain hunting and institutional participation helped sustain the rally.

Analysts said the rebound signals an improving appetite for Hong Kong equities, although investors remain watchful of external risks such as global monetary policy shifts and geopolitical developments.

Attention in the coming week will centre on earnings reports and macroeconomic indicators, which are expected to provide fresh cues for market direction.

data.title: Weekly Recap: South Korean Stocks Edge Higher As Tech Shares Support Gains
data.description:
South Korea’s stock market ended the trading week of April 20 to April 24, 2026, on a firmer note, with the benchmark Kospi posting modest gains as technology and export-driven counters lent support amid improving investor sentiment.

On the close of trading week, the benchmark Kospi slipped just 0.18 points to close at 6,475.63, narrowly missing a fresh record after touching 6,475.81 in the previous session.

Trading sentiment remained constructive through the week as investors responded positively to easing external uncertainties and stronger performances across regional markets. Technology shares, particularly chipmakers, were among the key contributors to the index’s advance.

While profit-taking emerged in select sectors toward the end of the week, overall market resilience was supported by expectations of stable corporate earnings and sustained export momentum.

Attention in the coming week will turn to earnings announcements and macroeconomic indicators, which are expected to provide fresh direction for Seoul equities.

data.title: Ringgit Opens Higher Against Dollar, Lower Against Majors
data.description:
The ringgit edged higher against the US dollar but was traded mostly lower against other major currencies in early trade on Monday amid subdued market sentiment ahead of the US Federal Reserve meeting later this week, Bernama reported. 

At 8.14 am, the local currency was marginally higher at 3.9610/9680 against the greenback, compared with 3.9630/9670 at Friday’s close.

SPI Asset Management managing partner Stephen Innes said the foreign exchange market is currently subdued, a typical pattern ahead of a US Federal Reserve meeting as traders assess whether the policy guidance will lean hawkish or dovish.

The Federal Open Market Committee meeting will be held on April 28 and 29. 

Innes said positioning has narrowed and conviction has eased, with market participants largely holding back until there is greater clarity.

On the geopolitical side, Innes said the lack of progress in the Iran peace talks is acting as a mild drag on risk sentiment and likely capped what could have been a broader dollar sell-off earlier in the week.

“That said, unless we see a meaningful military escalation that drives oil materially higher, the impact should stay contained. For now, I’d expect a modest bias toward ringgit appreciation, but nothing aggressive.

“The broader setup still leans toward range trading, as the dollar is unlikely to weaken meaningfully with oil holding around current levels. That should keep the downside in US dollar contained, limiting any sustained ringgit rally,” he told Bernama.

At the opening, the ringgit depreciated against a basket of major currencies.

It fell against the British pound to 5.3529/3624 from 5.3429/3483 at Friday’s close, declined against the Japanese yen to 2.4831/4876 from Friday’s 2.4808/4834 and eased against the euro to 4.6379/6461 from Friday’s 4.6312/6358.  

The local currency traded mixed against ASEAN peers.

It eased against the Singapore dollar to 3.1020/1078 from 3.1009/1043 at Friday’s close, and was slightly higher against the Thai baht to 12.2181/2465 from 12.2183/2363. 

It edged up against the Indonesian rupiah at 229.9/230.4 from 230.0/230.3 and was almost flat versus the Philippine peso at 6.52/6.54 from 6.52/6.53.

data.title: Bitcoin Nears US$80,000, Ethereum Extends Rally On Institutional Demand
data.description:
Bitcoin and Ethereum traded higher on April 27, with renewed institutional inflows and improving macro sentiment pushing the two largest cryptocurrencies toward key resistance levels.

Bitcoin hovered around US$78,600 to US$79,100 during the session on April 27, marking gains of roughly 1% to 2% over the previous day and placing the token within striking distance of the psychologically significant US$80,000 mark.

Ethereum also strengthened, trading near US$2,370 to US$2,390, extending its month-long recovery as investors rotated back into risk assets. The world’s second-largest cryptocurrency has gained more than 8% over the past month, supported by improving sentiment across digital assets.

Market watchers said Bitcoin’s rally is being underpinned by strong institutional accumulation, including sustained inflows into spot Bitcoin ETFs and fresh corporate treasury purchases.

Analysts cited by market reports noted that institutional demand has helped offset lingering macro concerns, with Bitcoin climbing above US$79,000 for the first time in weeks. However, the US$78,000-US$80,000 range is emerging as a crucial resistance zone where traders may begin taking profits.

Ethereum’s advance has been more measured but remains notable as the asset continues to outperform broader expectations after months of subdued trading. Recent forecasts suggest Ethereum’s resilience above the US$2,300 support band could pave the way for further upside if momentum holds.

The broader crypto market capitalisation stood near US$2.7 trillion on April 27, reflecting a steady rebound in investor confidence after heightened geopolitical uncertainty earlier in the month.

For now, traders are watching whether Bitcoin can decisively break through US$80,000, a move that could trigger the next leg of the rally, while Ethereum’s ability to hold above US$2,350 may determine whether altcoins continue to track higher in the near term.

data.title: Dollar Holds Steady As Markets Weigh Shaky US-Iran Diplomacy
data.description:
The dollar held steady on Monday as investors assessed uneven progress in United States-Iran negotiations aimed at ending a two-month conflict, with sentiment clouded by uncertainty over whether a lasting ceasefire can hold and the strategic Strait of Hormuz remaining effectively closed.

Geopolitical tensions stayed in focus after United States President Donald Trump cancelled a planned visit to Islamabad by his envoys, saying Iran could reach out if it wanted to negotiate an end to the war. The move followed reports that diplomatic efforts had stalled even as the ceasefire paused full-scale fighting that began with strikes in late February.

Sentiment briefly improved after Axios reported, citing sources, that Iran had sent a new proposal via Pakistani mediators covering reopening of the waterway and an end to hostilities, with nuclear talks pushed to a later stage. Markets, however, remained cautious as no formal agreement has been reached.

Currency markets reflected the wait-and-see tone. The euro was steady at $1.1724 while sterling traded at $1.3536. The dollar index stood at 98.491, holding recent gains after earlier safe-haven demand faded as hopes of a near-term resolution emerged.

Analysts noted markets may be underpricing risks. One senior financial analyst said confidence in progress appeared “perhaps even blase” and warned that “if it doesn’t the markets will have to re-price quite violently”.

Attention is also turning to central banks this week, with the Bank of Japan expected to keep rates unchanged but signal possible tightening later in the year. The yen remained weak at 159.51 per dollar, hovering near levels that could prompt intervention.

The Federal Reserve, European Central Bank and Bank of England are all expected to hold rates steady while assessing the inflation impact from higher energy prices.

Reuters

data.title: Singapore Stocks End Lower As Geopolitical Concerns Weigh On Sentiment
data.description:
Singapore equities closed lower on April 24, extending recent losses as investor caution deepened over geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and persistent inflation concerns.

The benchmark Straits Times Index (STI) declined 0.3% to 4,929.50 points, marking its third consecutive session in the red and its lowest level in nearly a month.

Market sentiment remained fragile as uncertainty surrounding US-Iran ceasefire talks and fears of oil supply disruptions kept investors on edge. Rising crude prices also added to inflation worries, fuelling expectations of tighter monetary policy in Singapore.

Most sectors traded lower, with weakness seen across financials, industrials and property stocks.

Among notable decliners were City Developments, SATS and UOB, as broader risk appetite softened.

For the week, the STI was on track for a 1.8% decline, ending a month-long stretch of gains as global volatility overshadowed regional resilience.

Investors will now turn their attention to upcoming economic data and central bank signals for clearer direction in the week ahead.

data.title: Straits Times Index Falls 0.46% At Start Of Trade
data.description:
Singapore shares opened lower, tracking a cautious regional mood as oil prices surged and geopolitical tensions between the United States and Iran intensified over the weekend.

The Straits Times Index (STI) slipped 0.46% to 4,899.98 in early trade as markets reacted to higher energy prices and weaker sentiment in US futures.

Across the broader market, trading remained relatively balanced with 141 gainers and 138 decliners. Total volume stood at 328.79 million shares, valued at S$253.93 million, reflecting muted participation at the open.

The weakness came after Brent crude climbed more than 2% to a three-week high of US$107.97 per barrel, driven by renewed concerns over supply disruptions linked to stalled US Iran negotiations and continued uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz.

Sentiment was further pressured after S&P 500 futures dipped 0.3% following record highs on Wall Street last week, suggesting investors were turning more cautious amid rising geopolitical risks. The US dollar also strengthened modestly against major currencies, reinforcing a broader risk-off tone.

US President Donald Trump said dialogue with Iran remained possible but under strict conditions, stating that any agreement would require Tehran to abandon nuclear ambitions. However, efforts to restart talks appeared to lose momentum after diplomatic engagements were disrupted over the weekend.

Elsewhere in Singapore’s market landscape, broader indices showed mixed performance with S-REIT and regional benchmark indices holding steady, while derivatives activity remained subdued in early trading.

data.title: Gainers Edge Out Losers As Tech Heavyweights Support Market Tone
data.description:
Stock movements on Bursa Malaysia ended mixed, with gainers outpacing losers amid selective buying in tech and industrials, though broader sentiment remained cautious.

Among the top gainers, Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd (MPI) soared RM1.50 to RM37.84 on brisk volume of 1.2 million shares, leading the pack, followed by Nestle (M) Bhd, which added RM1.30 to RM99.30.

VitroX Corp Bhd surged 54 sen to RM5.52 with high activity of 15.2 million shares, while UWC Bhd gained 42 sen to RM5.42 and Press Metal Aluminium Holdings Bhd rose 35 sen to RM8.42 on strong 21.7 million share volume.

On the downside, Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd led decliners, falling 40 sen to RM30.30, followed by Chin Tek Plantations Bhd, which slipped 30 sen to RM10.80.

Other notable losers included Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd, down 26 sen to RM32.60, United Plantations Bhd, which shed 26 sen to RM32.32 and Allianz Malaysia Bhd, lower by 22 sen to RM21.06.

Overall, the market’s positive tilt in gainers highlights pockets of strength in semiconductors and metals, with volumes pointing to focused investor interest.

data.title: Weekly Review: Singapore Stocks End Week Higher On Renewed Buying Interest
data.description:
Singapore’s stock market ended the trading week of April 20 to April 24, 2026, on a positive note, with the benchmark Straits Times Index (STI) gaining ground as investors returned to blue-chip counters amid improving regional sentiment.

The STI closed the week at 3,506.12, up 24.38 points, or 0.7%, from 3,481.74 at the start of the week, supported by gains in banking, industrial and property-related stocks.

Trading was largely upbeat through the week as investors tracked firmer global markets and easing concerns over geopolitical tensions. Banking heavyweights remained key drivers of the index’s performance, while selective buying in defensive counters helped sustain momentum.

Despite some profit-taking toward the end of the week, the broader market remained resilient, with analysts pointing to steady institutional participation and confidence in Singapore’s economic outlook.

Market watchers said the STI’s ability to remain above the 3,500-point mark reflects underlying strength, although external risks such as global interest rate expectations and commodity price swings may continue to shape sentiment.

Looking ahead, investors are expected to focus on upcoming corporate earnings releases and macroeconomic indicators for fresh market direction as Singapore equities maintain a cautiously optimistic tone.

data.title: Wall Street Hits New Highs As Chip Rally Powers S&P, Nasdaq To Records
data.description:
US stocks ended mixed on April 24, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite closing at fresh record highs as hopes for renewed US-Iran peace talks and a powerful semiconductor rally lifted investor sentiment.

Reuters reported that the benchmark S&P 500 rose 0.8% to 7,165.08, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite surged 1.63% to 24,836.60. The Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 0.16% to 49,230.71.

Markets drew support from reports of possible diplomatic efforts involving Pakistan to restart negotiations between the US and Iran, raising hopes of easing geopolitical tensions that have weighed on sentiment.

Semiconductor stocks led the advance, with the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index jumping 4.32% for its 18th straight gain. Intel soared 23.65% to a record US$82.57 after issuing a stronger-than-expected second-quarter revenue forecast.

Other chipmakers also rallied, with Advanced Micro Devices and Arm Holdings each gaining about 14%, while Nvidia climbed 4.32% to another record close.

The S&P 500 technology sector rose 2.46%, making it the top-performing sector of the day.

For the week, the S&P 500 gained 0.55% and the Nasdaq advanced 1.5%, extending both indexes’ winning streak to four straight weeks. The Dow, however, fell 0.44%, snapping a three-week run of gains.

Investor focus now turns to next week’s Federal Reserve meeting, with markets increasingly betting on a rate cut in December amid shifting expectations around US monetary policy.

data.title: Market Recap: Bursa Malaysia Ends Higher For The Week As Risk Appetite Improves
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia closed this past trading week on a firmer footing, with the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rising 16.70 points, or 0.98%, to 1,720.34 from 1,703.64 at the start of the week, reflecting improving investor sentiment amid easing geopolitical concerns and steady buying in heavyweight counters.

The local bourse began the week on a positive note, tracking stronger regional markets as global risk appetite improved. Momentum carried through midweek, supported by renewed interest in banking and industrial-linked stocks, before a mild pullback on April 24 as investors locked in gains ahead of the weekend.

Despite the softer finish in the final session, where the FBM KLCI slipped 1.36 points, the broader weekly trend remained constructive. The session on April 23 marked the strongest session of the week, with the benchmark index climbing to 1,721.70, buoyed by optimism surrounding an extension of the US-Iran ceasefire and resilient crude oil prices.

Market breadth remained broadly healthy, underpinned by buying interest in index heavyweights such as banking names and industrial products counters. The improved tone in equities also reflected confidence in Malaysia’s macroeconomic outlook, with investors positioning for continued earnings resilience in the corporate sector.

Analysts noted that while near-term volatility may persist due to fluctuations in oil prices and global geopolitical developments, Bursa Malaysia’s ability to sustain gains above the 1,700-point level signals underlying market strength.

For the week, the FBM KLCI’s advance reinforced its steady upward trajectory in 2026, as investors continued to favour fundamentally strong counters amid a cautiously optimistic global backdrop. Attention now shifts to upcoming corporate earnings releases and external market cues, which are expected to shape trading sentiment in the week ahead.

data.title: Seoul Shares End Flat As Defence Gains Offset Tech Weakness
data.description:
South Korean equities ended largely unchanged on April 24, as gains in defence and shipbuilding stocks helped offset losses in heavyweight tech and auto counters.

The benchmark Korea Composite Stock Price Index (Kospi) slipped just 0.18 points to close at 6,475.63, narrowly missing a fresh record after touching 6,475.81 in the previous session.

Trading was active but orderly, with volume reaching 871 million shares worth 29.82 trillion won (US$20.08 billion). Advancers outpaced decliners 619 to 236, highlighting selective buying beneath the flat headline index.

Sentiment was capped by lingering uncertainty over US-Iran negotiations and broader geopolitical tensions, which kept risk appetite in check.

Tech and auto stocks led the downturn. Market bellwether Samsung Electronics fell 2.23% to 219,500 won, while SK hynix eased 0.24% to 1,222,000 won.

Automakers also weakened, with Hyundai Motor dropping 3.57% to 513,400 won and Kia falling 3.16% to 153,400 won. Refiners were mixed, as SK Innovation slipped 0.23% and S-Oil declined 1.16%.

In contrast, defence stocks attracted strong buying interest. Hanwha Aerospace rose 2.67% to 1,463,000 won, while LIG Nex1 jumped 2.38% to 989,000 won, reflecting continued investor rotation into geopolitical hedge plays.

Overall, the market closed in consolidation mode after recent record highs, with investors balancing defensive positioning against ongoing volatility in global risk factors.

data.title: Nvidia Retakes 5T Market Cap Crown, Stock Hits Record High
data.description:
Nvidia retook the $5 trillion market cap crown on Friday, according to Yahoo Finance data, as a powerful rally in chip stocks encouraged by Intel earnings and a nuclear power deal with Oklo boosted shares.

The stock gained as much as 4.2% on Friday, adding more than $200 billion in value to the company’s market capitalization, which rose as high as $5.12 trillion.

Nvidia is now worth $1 trillion more in market cap than the second-largest company, Alphabet.

Nvidia also notched a record closing high. At $208 per share, the stock is just off its all-time intraday high of $212.19, reached on Oct. 29, 2025.Nvidia did not start the year on a sure footing. Through the first three months of the year, shares of the artificial intelligence leader were down 6.4%.

But April has provided a compelling comeback story. Over the past month, Nvidia stock has risen 20%, helped by the broader rally in semiconductor stocks.

data.title: Gold Price Downward Trajectory Likely To Settle At US$4,600
data.description:
COMEX Gold prices faced a fresh wave of selling pressure, as the precious metal retreated below a key moving average, signaling that bears have regained control of the technical landscape.

According to a technical analysis report by RHB Investment Bank, the commodity dropped USD29.00 to settle at USD4,724.00. The session saw gold open at USD4,759.20 and reach an intraday high of USD4,771.30 before sliding to a low of USD4,680.10.

RHB noted that the bearish price action was particularly significant as it forced the metal below its 20-day Simple Moving Average (SMA) line. This downward movement was mirrored by the Relative Strength Index (RSI), which also dipped, suggesting that bearish momentum is picking up pace.

“The precious metal is likely to pull back towards the USD4,600 level to form a consolidation base,” the report stated, adding that the overall technical setup currently gives the “upper hand” to the bears.

The downward trajectory is further reinforced by the 50-day SMA line, which continues to trend lower. Analysts highlighted that this line is currently acting as a formidable overhead resistance, capping any potential attempts at a price rebound.

Given the negative technical indicators, RHB has maintained its “negative” trading bias and recommended that investors maintain their short positions.

  • Immediate Resistance: The USD4,800 psychological mark remains a key hurdle for bulls.
  • Support Levels: While the USD4,600 mark is the immediate target for a consolidation base, a break below this could see further testing of long-term support zones.

The current weakness in gold comes amid a complex global economic environment where shifting interest rate expectations and a strengthening US dollar continue to weigh on non-yielding assets. For now, technical analy

data.title: Bursa Ends Mixed As Tech Stocks Lead Mid-Cap Gains
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended the trading session mixed with the FBM KLCI closing slightly lower while broader market indices posted gains.

The FBM KLCI slipped 1.36 points or 0.08% to 1,720.34, reflecting mild weakness among index-linked blue chips.

In contrast, broader benchmarks ended firmer. The FBM70 rose 56.18 points or 0.31% to 18,072.43 while the FBM Emas added 9.56 points or 0.08% to 12,715.72. The FBM Emas Shariah gained 21.25 points or 0.17% to 12,586.61 and the F4GBM Index increased 0.97 points or 0.09% to 1,027.13.

Market breadth showed mixed trading with select mid-cap and technology-related stocks attracting stronger interest.

Among the most actively traded counters were VS, Zetrix, Luster, MEGAFB and AMS.

Top gainers were led by MPI, which rose 1.50 to 37.84, followed by Nestle, Vitrox, UWC and PMETAL.

On the losing side, F&N led declines, followed by Chin Teck, Dutch Lady, UTD Plantations and Allianz.

data.title: Bursa Opens Higher As Banking And Tech Stocks Lead Gains
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened higher, with broad-based gains across major indices as investors tracked global markets unsettled by geopolitical tensions and rising oil prices.

The FBM KLCI rose 10.62 points or 0.62% to 1,730.96 in early trade, reflecting stronger sentiment across heavyweight counters.

The FBM70 index advanced 138.47 points or 0.77% to 18,210.90 while the FBM Emas climbed 80.38 points or 0.63% to 12,796.10. The FBM Emas Shariah gained 100.29 points or 0.80% to 12,686.90, and the F4GBM Index increased 6.54 points or 0.64% to 1,033.67.

Market breadth was positive with most key indices trading higher at the open, supported by selective buying in index-linked stocks and mid-cap counters.

Sentiment was shaped by a cautious global backdrop after Brent crude surged more than 2% to a three-week high of US$107.97 per barrel, driven by ongoing supply concerns linked to stalled US Iran negotiations and disruptions around key Gulf shipping routes.

US stock futures also slipped 0.3%, suggesting some profit-taking after Wall Street’s recent record highs. The US dollar strengthened slightly against major currencies, reflecting a mild risk-off tone across global markets.

Among active counters, Luster led trading volumes, followed by VS, JSB, AMS and VELESTO, while top gainers included Nestle, MPI, PMETAL, TM and Vitrox. Decliners were more limited, led by Chin Teck, Padini, BAT, Kossan and SOP.

data.title: YTL Corp In Main Driving Seat
data.description:
MBSB Investment Bank Bhd (MBSB Research) has maintained its BUY call on YTL Corp Bhd with a higher target price of RM3.44 from RM3.26.

Analysts said the group remains well-positioned to benefit from infrastructure expansion, data centre demand and a stronger construction cycle supported by Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone-driven activity and improving cement fundamentals.

The research house said the revised sum-of-the-parts valuation incorporates updated assumptions for YTL Power International and Malayan Cement, reflecting stronger long-term visibility across the group’s utilities and building materials divisions.

It added that YTL continues to be a key proxy for infrastructure-led growth while its strategic push into data centres and renewable energy enhances earnings resilience.

MBSB Research highlighted that the group’s cement arm is expected to remain a major beneficiary of rising construction demand from warehouses, residential developments and large-scale data centre projects.

The upcoming Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone is also expected to act as an additional catalyst for regional industrial activity.

At the same time, YTL Corporation through YTL Cement has launched an unconditional mandatory takeover offer for Concrete Engineering Products Bhd at RM2.60 per share after increasing its stake to 53.49% for RM103.8 million.

The offer, which closes on May 13, 2026, could involve up to RM90.2 million in additional consideration if fully accepted.

MBSB Research said the acquisition strengthens YTL Cement’s vertical integration strategy by expanding its precast capabilities into spun concrete piles and poles, complementing its existing cement and aggregates operations.

It noted that demand for precast solutions is expected to remain firm given efficiency needs across infrastructure, industrial and data centre construction.

The research house added that the offer price implies a FY25 price-to-earnings ratio of about 10 times based on CEPCO’s core earnings of RM19.5 million, describing it as fair and strategically accretive.

No changes were made to earnings estimates while the higher target price reflects updated valuations across key subsidiaries.

As of 10.48 am, the stock price slips 0.50% to RM2.01.

data.title: Stock Picks: Keyfield And Perdana Petroleum
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Keyfield International Bhd and Perdana Petroleum Bhd as stocks with bullish technical momentum, following recent breakouts supported by strong trading volume.

The research house said Keyfield International is poised to extend its upside after breaking above the RM1.70 resistance level, indicating a bullish setup. It noted that the counter could next test resistance at RM1.83, followed by RM1.95 if momentum persists. However, RHB Research cautioned that a decline below the RM1.55 support level would invalidate the bullish outlook.

For Perdana Petroleum, RHB Research said the stock is also on a positive trajectory after surpassing the RM0.19 resistance on rising volume, signalling sustained buying interest. The counter is expected to test the RM0.21 level, with further upside towards RM0.225.

The research house added that a drop below RM0.175 would indicate a potential resumption of the correction phase for Perdana Petroleum.

data.title: HSIF Inches Up, Yet Keep Eyes On 25,100 Support
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained its negative trading bias on Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF), advising traders to stay on short positions despite a recent rebound supported by key moving averages.

The research house noted that HSIF gained 44 points to close at 25,965 points last Friday after testing a low of 25,629 points before rebounding to an intraday high of 26,015 points. The index extended gains in evening trading, rising a further 76 points to last trade at 26,041 points.

RHB said the latest price action suggests that both the 20-day and 50-day simple moving average lines are acting as strong support levels. As long as the index holds above the 50-day SMA, it may continue to climb towards the 26,600-point resistance.

However, the research house cautioned that a break below the 20-day SMA line could trigger fresh selling pressure, potentially pushing the index down towards the 25,100-point support level. A deeper decline could see it testing 24,500 points.

Despite the recent upward movement, RHB reiterated its view for traders to maintain short positions initiated at 26,367 points, with a stop-loss set at 26,600 points to manage risk.
